breakdown: Naming: Test function names are well-descriptive (test_process_action_stream_infers_failure, test_runtime_bootstrap_skips_numpy_downgrade, etc.). Variable names like missing_modules, installed_modules, commands are specific. Error Handling: Tests verify error responses include error/status_code, but most source code error handling is not visible - only test assertions confirming desired behavior. Type Safety: Strong typing in tests (list[list[str]], set[str], etc.), but cannot evaluate type safety of actual code changes since only test additions are shown. Architecture: Test structure properly separates concerns (tests/scripts/), uses monkeypatch to isolate modules. Source code architecture not visible. Test Coverage: Multiple comprehensive tests added covering failure scenarios, runtime bootstrap edge cases (numpy version, cache invalidation, fallback packages, external server runs). The test file adds boundary-aware test detection validation with proper assertions. Documentation: Test files have minimal docstrings which is acceptable for tests. Test function names are self-documenting.
key_changes: Added tests/scripts/test_loc_script.py for loc.sh boundary-aware test detection
